Defendant Morning Chuang'S Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 525

Defendant Morning Chuang'S Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Attorneys S. Edmond El Dabe and Jonathan Ritter for Plaintiffs Roche Hahan and Marielisa Villasenor the moving party.
Attorney Alan F. Hunter appearing for Defendant Morning Chuang the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of the Plaintiff's Motion to Tax Costs, the Court rules as follows;

the judgment will accrue interest on damages at the rate of 10% per annum from the date of judgment (December 7, 2017) to the date of payment.

The Court granted Defendant's Motion to Tax Costs, in part. The court awarded costs of $56,137.98.

After further oral argument and considering all the briefing, the Court found the March 29, 2016 C.C.P. 998 offers served by Plaintiffs on Defendant were valid and effective, with prejudgment interest (pursuant to Civil Code 3291) on damages running from the date of said offers.

The Court awarded prejudgment interest in the amount of $403,647.12 to Plaintiff ROCHE HAHAN and $210,289.32 to Plaintiff MARIELISA VILLASENOR.

All statutory deadlines that accrued prior to March 19, 2018 are to run from the date of the original judgment of December 7, 2017.

Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Cecilia Castellanos, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Recorded. (525)
